reg_title: Morale, Welfare, and Recreation Military Morale, Welfare, and

chunks.text:
This regulation establishes policy, prescribes procedures, and assigns responsibilities for Army Morale, Welfare, and
Recreation (MWR) programs and nonappropriated fund instrumentalities (NAFIs) encompassing the garrison MWR
operating entities and includes NAFIs of the Department of Defense (DoD) established in accordance with Army
policies herein.
Required and related publications and prescribed and referenced forms are listed in appendix A.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 1 > 1-3. Explanation of abbreviations and terms
chunks.text: Abbreviations and special terms used in this regulation are listed in the glossary.
Responsibilities are listed in chapter 2.

The records management requirement for all record numbers, associated forms, and reports required by this regulation
are addressed in the Army Records Retention Schedule-Army (RRS-A). Detailed information for all related record
numbers, forms, and reports are located in ARIMS/RRS-A at https://www.arims.army.mil. If any record numbers,
forms, and reports are not current, addressed, and/or published correctly in ARIMS/RRS-A, see DA Pam 25-403 for
guidance.

Army NAFIs/entities, NAFIs operated by the Army for DoD agencies, and MWR programs are established, main-
tained, and disestablished under sole authority of this regulation. Department of Defense policies contained in DoD
Directive (DoDD) 1015.2 and DoD Instructions (DoDIs) 1015.10, 1015.13, and 1015.15 are implemented in this
regulation.

The Army and Air Force Exchange Service (AAFES) is a joint service NAFI operated and established by au-
thority of the respective service Secretaries. It is exempt from the provisions of this regulation unless otherwise spec-
ified herein or in AAFES policies.

The following funds and related activities are exempt from the provisions of this regulation unless stated other-
wise in other applicable regulations or herein. The appropriate governing regulation, where applicable, is indicated in
parentheses.
Army Emergency Relief funds (AR 930-4).
Prisoner of war funds.
Patient trust funds.
Prisoner personal deposit funds.
Donor deposit funds.
Funds established for the benefit of civilian employees of the Corps of Engineers.

Contractor and subcontractor funds on Department of the Army (DA) garrisons, including funds established
for contractor employees and concessionaire funds.
Funds of labor union locals, veterans’ organizations, banks, and credit unions.

All funds accounted for under U.S. Treasury symbols and resources acquired with appropriated funds (APFs),
such as the Trust Revolving Fund Account and Army Commissary Surcharge Fund.
Funds established by private organizations (POs) authorized to operate on Army garrisons (AR 210-22).
U.S. Military Academy (USMA) Cadet Mess Ration Fund and personal trust funds (AR 210-3).
U.S. Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home personal trust funds.
Funds generated by blind vendors operating under authority of the Randolph-Sheppard Act (AR 210-25).
Chaplain’s Nonappropriated Funds (AR 165-1).
The U.S. Army Nonappropriated Fund Employee Retirement Plan Trust.
The U.S. Army Nonappropriated Fund Employee 401(k) Savings Plan Trust.
Department of the Army Welfare Fund (AR 230-3).
Informal funds (AR 600-20).
Civilian Welfare Funds and Post Restaurant Funds (AR 215-7).

Nonappropriated fund instrumentalities are classified into six program groups and further classified into three funding
categories. Chapter 3 outlines the NAFI structure.

The Army MWR program is a quality-of-life program that directly supports readiness by providing a variety of
community, Soldier, and Family support programs, activities, and services. Included are social, fitness, recreational,
educational, and other programs and activities that enhance community life, foster Soldier and unit readiness, promote
mental and physical fitness, and generally provide a working and living environment that attracts and retains quality
Soldiers.

The range of MWR programs offered at Army garrisons is based on the needs of authorized patrons who work
and reside there. Programs are managed by garrison commanders within the framework of authorized and available
APFs and nonappropriated funds (NAFs). Nonappropriated funds are those funds that are locally generated by MWR
programs or provided by Region directors and/or the Army Morale, Welfare, and Recreation Fund (AMWRF). AAFES
dividends are also a source of NAFs.

Each MWR program is classified by category. Categories are determined by their effect on the military mission
and their ability to generate revenue. Chapter 3 outlines the three categories of MWR programs.

Morale, Welfare, and Recreation programs are designed to meet the needs of the garrison community. This
includes Soldiers, units, retirees, civilian employees, and Families.
Morale, Welfare, and Recreation programs are prioritized based on their impact on readiness.

Morale, Welfare, and Recreation programs support readiness, well-being, recruiting, and retention of highly
qualified Soldiers.

Nonappropriated funds are returned to Soldiers, their Families, and other authorized patrons by providing needed
MWR programs, services, and capital improvements.

Program management is based on long-range planning, generation of NAF resources, and application of author-
ized APF resources to meet current operating requirements while providing for reinvestment in programs, facilities,
and equipment.
The MWR program-
Supports combat readiness and effectiveness.
Supports recruitment and retention of quality personnel.

Provides leisure time activities, which support a quality of life commensurate with generally accepted American
values.
Promotes and maintains the mental and physical well-being of authorized personnel.
Fosters community pride, Soldier morale, and Family wellness and promotes unit esprit de corps.
Eases the impact of unique aspects of military life, such as frequent relocations and deployment.
Responsibilities

Establish, maintain, and disestablish Army NAFIs/entities. This authority is delegated within the Army as pro-
vided for in this regulation (chap 3) and other pertinent regulations, with the exception that authority to establish joint
NAFIs is not further delegated.
Coordinate on joint service matters with the Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness.

Designate the Assistant Chief of Staff for Installation Management (ACSIM) as the Army Staff (ARSTAF)
proponent and focal point for all MWR programs and NAFIs.

Designate financial oversight of APF and NAF to the Assistant Secretary of the Army for Financial Management
and Comptroller (ASA (FM&C)), and responsibility for MWR, NAFIs/entities, and personnel to the Assistant Secre-
tary of the Army for Manpower and Reserve Affairs (ASA (M&RA)).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-2. The Assistant Chief of Staff for Installation Management
chunks.text:
Serve as the ARSTAF proponent for MWR and related programs, NAF and APF management of MWR pro-
grams and NAFIs, and Armywide MWR and NAFI policy.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-2. The Assistant Chief of Staff for Installation Management
chunks.text:
Exercise ARSTAF supervision over the Installation Management Command (IMCOM) and Family and Morale,
Welfare and Recreation Command (FMWRC) in matters pertaining to Army MWR APFs and NAFs and NAFIs.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-2. The Assistant Chief of Staff for Installation Management
chunks.text: Endorse, promote, and ensure compliance of Army MWR baseline standards.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-2. The Assistant Chief of Staff for Installation Management
chunks.text:
Articulate funding requirements identified by Army MWR baseline standards to the Secretary of the Army,
DoD, and Congress.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Act as single integrator and synchronizer with IMCOM Regions, the ARSTAF/ACSIM, FMWRC functional
proponents, and major Army commands (MACOMs), as needed, to resolve execution issues.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
In coordination with FMWRC, assist in the development, integration, and reporting of Army MWR baseline
standards.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Fund all MWR manpower authorizations, contractors, and requirements coded as MWR Utilization, Support
and Accountability (USA) Uniform Funding and Management (UFM) funding practice reimbursable (in the appropri-
ate Management Decision Evaluation Packages (MDEPs), to include Child Development Services (QCCS), Youth
Services (QYDP), Soldier MWR (QDPC), and HQ overhead (QMIS).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
In accordance with this regulation and c, fund authorized NAF accounting costs (for NAFIs/entities) incurred in
support of MWR operations.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Comply with congressional and DoD guidance to fund valid APF requirements with APFs through maximum
use of MWR USA or UFM funding practices.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Promote and evaluate consistency, efficiency, predictability, and best business practices of MWR programs
across IMCOM Regions.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Distribute congressional authorizations and appropriated fund monies for MWR programs in coordination with
FMWRC.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Provide oversight, management, and accountability for MWR programs. Ensure adherence to applicable law
and DoD/HQDA policies and regulations.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Receive and allocate NAF resources generated by other than direct garrison operations, to include items such as
Army Banking and Investment Fund interest, garrison share of AAFES revenue, Army Simplified Dividend, or Army
Recreation Machine Program (ARMP) revenue, in accordance with published financial management guidance.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Manage MWR Funds, as required.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Uniformly implement MWR programs and policies.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Review and approve APF budgets and provide program objective memorandum (POM) submissions to ensure
installations’ assess requirements and to promote equitable distribution of resources.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Approve installation-generated NAF budgets and submit them in accordance with annual budget guidance.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Track and analyze program performance and budget execution relative to established MWR baseline and Instal-
lation Status Report III standards.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Conduct accreditation, certification, inspection, and assistance programs for garrisons; use interregional program
specialists and FMWRC assets where appropriate; and regional proponent support for fire, engineers, and safety for
Regional Child and Youth Evaluation Teams.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Develop centralized NAF contracting support where and when feasible and provide oversight of NAF procure-
ment offices.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Provide oversight and accountability of the MWR program centrally managed management information systems
(MISs).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Execute MWR Management Control Program and Risk Management Program in accordance with AR 11-2.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Coordinate, review, and approve garrison NAF major construction requirements and capital purchases and minor
construction (CPMC) above garrison approval level in accordance with chapter 15 of this regulation and AR 420-1.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Coordinate, review, and approve garrison military construction, Army (MCA) designs and projects to ensure
compliance with Army standard designs and best practice principles.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
Support MWR requirements for deployment/mobilization and contingency operations, to include designation of
emergency essential (E-E) civilians, as appropriate.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Establish working group or executive committee for MWR program oversight.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text: Serve as proponent for MWR MCA requirements in coordination with the engineers.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
The Installation Management Command Southeast Region will support the Army National Guard and USAR for
unit fund actions and will effect necessary liaison to accomplish this support.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-3. The Commander, Installation Management Command
chunks.text:
The Installation Management Command Northeast Region will coordinate funding requests for MWR programs
of Army school commandants.
Ensure adherence to applicable law and DoD/Headquarters (HQ) DA policies and regulations.
Plan, manage, fund, and operate MWR programs and services based on Army baseline standards.

Develop annual NAF budgets and manage APF MDEPs (QCCS, QYDP, QDPC, and OSD funding) and NAF
resources. Ensure programs and activities are managed and operated on a sound financial basis, are in compliance
with Army policy, and are consistent with established financial performance, program performance, and facility stand-
ards and MWR baseline standards.

Conduct annual program reviews, customer needs assessments, and annual updates to the MWR 5-year garrison
plans and strategic business plan.
Ensure personnel are properly trained in customer service and program specific competencies.

Coordinate with servicing Civilian Personnel Advisory Center (CPAC) to ensure that NAF employees and or-
ganizations receive timely support.
Perform NAF procurement as authorized by policy in AR 215-4 and other applicable policies.
Execute CPMC projects in accordance with approved NAF budget.
Employ public affairs and marketing and advertising resources to inform population about MWR programs.

Identify APF requirements based on DoD/Army/MWR program baseline standards for inclusion in the garrison
base operations budget.
Plan, develop, and submit MCA requirements.
Prepare and update plans for operation during mobilization and contingency.
Plan for MWR support during mobilization, wartime, and contingency operations.
Validate garrison and unit MWR personnel requirements identified in the E-E civilian program.

Resource and assist units in the identification and procurement of MWR/athletic and recreation (A&R) deploy-
ment kits.

Enforce use of MWR centrally managed MIS (see chap 13). Implement, operate, and provide technical support
for approved MWR management information systems.

Support all military units, provide normal garrison support for tenant organizations, and provide support for the
USAR, National Guard, and Accessions Command. Outside the continental United States (OCONUS) may support
other Government agencies. Implement interservice support agreements, when required.

Provide authorized local administrative and logistical support to FMWRC concert series and other touring
groups and shows scheduled through Armed Forces entertainment outlined in AR 215-6.

Review inspection results and accidents involving MWR activities, programs, and patrons and initiate corrective
action as necessary to prevent personal injury and property damage.
Ensure statutory labor relations obligations are met, where applicable, in implementation of this regulation.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Develop program guidance, standards, and procedures to implement approved Army policies.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Sit on or designate a representative to sit on the AAFES and other BODs, as directed.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Develop plans, strategies, programs, policies, and operating guidance for the management and administration of
a full range of MWR programs (see chap 3, fig 3-1). Create and coordinate the MWR programs’ strategic plans.
Disseminate guidance through the IMCOM for synchronization, integration, and implementation.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Develop organization and operation agreements or memorandum of agreement (MOA) to define procedures and
responsibilities for management, oversight, and support of MWR programs in coordination with the IMCOM.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Support commanders of deployed units with MWR planning and/or execution of MWR in accordance with FM
1-0 (see chap 9).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Formulate and promulgate best business practices in coordination with IMCOM.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Serve as the point of contact (POC) for congressional and higher HQ inquiries.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Develop and execute public affairs, command information, media relations, and marketing and advertising plans.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Administer and execute MWR internal review and management control programs in accordance with ARs 11-2
and 11-7.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Act as the primary action and liaison official for external audits of MWR programs.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Continually review the MWR programs to quantify and profile requirements.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Research, test, evaluate, and establish alternative business strategies and identify ways to improve business pro-
cesses and program practices.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Support IMCOM with management and technical assistance.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Develop and execute career management strategies and programs to develop Career Field 51 employees and
provide input and recommendations for CF 34 (Librarians) and CF 27 (Housing Management), and operate the MWR
Career Referral Program.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Operate and administer health, life, retirement, savings, and other benefit programs for NAF employees.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Develop and deliver training for MWR program managers. Centrally fund training approved for inclusion in the
MWR Master Training Program.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Administer and execute NAF major construction (MC) program to include category C public private ventures
(PPV).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Develop standards and exercise oversight for the NAF procurement program (AR 215-4).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Execute NAF procurement in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Develop accreditation, certification, and inspection programs for MWR programs for IMCOM implementation.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Develop program standards, benchmarks, outcome criteria, and operating guidance for management of MWR
programs for IMCOM implementation.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Operate the Armed Forces Recreation Centers (AFRCs) and ARMP.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Oversee, manage, and control the Army MWR MISs and the MWR Configuration Control Board process.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Manage MWR financial management process and provide necessary information for IMCOM synchronization
of resources and issues.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Operate and administer common support NAF financial management functions such as banking, investment, and
insurance and establish investment oversight groups for each activity that invests NAFs.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Administer the Army MWR and Army Recreation Machine (ARM) trust funds. The AMWRF is the successor-
in-interest to IMCOM Regions and other designated NAFIs.
aa. Review and approve requests for establishment of all Army NAFIs/entities and DoD agencies requesting NAFI
establishment under the Army’s policies and appoint advisory groups for DA-level NAFIs.
bb. Serve as the POC for joint service policy pertaining to the AAFES, to include recommending policy to the
ACSIM for, and publication of, applicable joint service regulations. Serve as DA POC for and represent the Army on
joint service committees and working groups, to include DoD MWR committees. Serve as executive agent responsible
to the OSD for the annual consolidated MWR Financial and Personnel Management Report (see chap 16, sec VI).
cc. Serve as DoD POC for DoDI 1015.09 and Army POC for funding Boy and Girl Scout programs overseas.
dd. Designate Army representatives to the Inter-Service Sports Committee, the U.S. Olympic Committee, the Am-
ateur Athletic Union, and other national sports governing bodies as appropriate and direct and control Army partici-
pation in interservice, national, and international events and Army World Class Athlete Program (WCAP) (see chap
8).
ee. Obtain copyright and royalty clearances, as required, for Armywide sponsored entertainment events.
ff. In coordination with the U.S. Army Soldier Support Center and U.S. Army Training and Doctrine Command
(TRADOC), develop MWR doctrine, policy, and operations procedural guidance for mobilization, contingencies, and
wartime. Delineate unit/individual responsibilities.
gg. Produce the U.S. Army Soldier Show.
hh. Develop specifications and standards for procurement and distribution of library materials and administer a
supplemental acquisition program.
ii. Integrate Army Pest Management Program requirements into Army MWR programs and NAFIs in accordance
with AR 200-5.
jj. With regard to NAFs, act as Army proponent for all aspects of NAF financial management, to include-

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Publishing annual NAF budget guidance.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Reviewing IMCOM consolidated IMCOM Region budgets.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Providing primary oversight for NAF accounting procedures in coordination with Defense Finance and Ac-
counting Service (DFAS).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Publishing and submit appropriate reports and trend analyses as required or necessary.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Maintaining NAF database for management information.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Establishing liaison with DFAS on accounting policy and systems.
kk. With regard to APFs and in accordance with Army Planning, Programming, Budget Execution System guid-
ance-

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Orchestrate the development of Armywide MWR POM requirements.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Coordinate with IMCOM to ensure baseline standards and Army priorities are addressed.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Serve as functional POC for all MWR in MDEP, POM, and military construction processes.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Present and defend POM requirements to Installation Program Evaluation Group (and others as appropriate).

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Work directly with IMCOM, ACSIM Resource Integration Office, and ARSTAF to ensure MWR issues are
appropriately addressed and recorded in applicable databases.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Assist the IMCOM with maintaining the Army MWR programs’ priority list of unfinanced requirements.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Recommend to the Army Budget Office (ASA (FM&C)) and IMCOM the allocation of resources by MDEP
and IMCOM Region/garrison.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text:
Monitor the execution of MWR MDEP resources. Take appropriate action to ensure programming, budgeting,
and execution objectives are met.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-5. Commander, Family and Morale, Welfare and Recreation Command
chunks.text: Publish and submit appropriate reports and trend analyses as required or necessary.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 2 > 2-6. Heads of Headquarters, Department of the Army agencies and commanders of Army
chunks.text:
commands, installations, and activities
The heads of the above agencies, commands, installations, and activities need to have knowledge of AR 340-21, and
AR 25-55.

Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality Structure
NAFIs and their programs are classified into one of six program groups. Within each group, programs are further
classified into one of three funding categories. The funding categories are the basis for APF and NAF authorizations
in appendix D.
Section I
Nonappropriated Fund Instrumentality Groups

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 3 > 3-1. Program Group I: Military morale, recreation and welfare programs
chunks.text:
All military MWR programs, to include Child Development Services and School Age Care, are included within this
group. Policies pertaining to these programs are contained throughout this regulation and AR 608-10. Military MWR
programs are classified into three funding categories outlined in section II of this chapter.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 3 > 3-2. Program Group II: Armed Forces exchanges
chunks.text:
Policies pertaining to this group of NAFIs are contained in AR 215-8. Exchanges are classified as a category C pro-
gram.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 3 > 3-3. Program Group III: Civilian morale, recreation and welfare programs
chunks.text:
Policies pertaining to this group of NAFIs are contained in AR 215-7. Civilian MWR programs are classified as a
category C program.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 3 > 3-4. Program Group IV: Lodging Program supplemental mission funds
chunks.text:
Army lodging is an APF mission. The Army Lodging Program Supplemental Mission Fund is a NAFI and receives
APF and NAF support comparable to a category C program.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 3 > 3-5. Program Group V: Supplemental mission funds
chunks.text: Policies pertaining to this group of NAFIs are contained in this regulation and various other regulations identified in

receive funding support comparable to category C programs. Nonappropriated fund instrumentalities in this group
include, but are not limited to-
School Lunch Program.
Museums.
Vehicle Registration Fund.
Cadet Activities Fund.
Athletic Association Fund.
U.S. Disciplinary Barracks.
Stars and Stripes.
Military Treatment Lodging Facilities.

chunks.heading_path: Chapter 5 > 3-6. Program Group VI: Special purpose central funds
chunks.text:
These funds are sole providers of a consolidated NAF function, such as construction, accounting, procurement, per-
sonnel administration, employee health and life insurance, and risk management. These are HQ-level programs. Ex-
penses of these funds are generally recovered through premiums or assessments charged or offset against earnings
prior to distribution. These funds are authorized the same APF and NAF support authorized for category C programs.
Centrally administered NAFIs are shown in table 3-1.
Table 3-1
Listing of centrally administered Department of the Army nonappropriated fund instrumentalities-Continued
NAFI: Army Morale Welfare and Recreation Fund
SNN: BA1
Scope: Ultimate successor-in-interest to all Army operating NAFIs. Major financing agent to Army NAFMC program.
NAFI: Army Medical/Life Fund
SNN: BAA
Scope: Repository of the assets of the U.S. Army NAF Group Insurance Plan. Collects group health, life, and dental insurance premi-
ums from NAF employees and employers; contracts with various providers of coverage on behalf of NAF employees; pays premiums
and service fees to direct providers of health, dental, and life insurance coverage on behalf of NAF employees; pays claims expenses
and direct and indirect general administrative expenses of health and dental insurance incurred by the plans; and provides general
administrative services to the plans.
NAFI: Army Banking and Investment Fund
SNN: BA9
Scope: Provides a centralized banking and investment vehicle for NAFIs.
NAFI: Army Central Insurance Fund
SNN: BAC
Scope: Supports the NAF Risk Management Program, a centralized property and casualty insurance program for U.S. Army
NAFIs/entities.
NAFI: Hospitality Cash Management Fund
SNN: BAJ
Scope: Provides centralized cash management for Armed Forces Recreation Centers and finances capital purchase and minor con-
struction requirement and other investment initiatives.
NAFI: Army Recreation Machine Fund
SNN: BA8
Scope: Provides amusement and gaming machine entertainment to authorized patrons, from which profits are used to fund the MWR
program worldwide.
NAFI: Hale Koa Hotel Armed Forces Recreation Center, Honolulu, Hawaii
SNN: BKD
Scope: Provides a first-class, full-service hotel and adjunct recreational facilities and programs at affordable prices for eligible pa-
trons.

trons.
